steps.title: Identify what the conclusion predicts and where to look in the data
steps.content: The researchers concluded that people reviewing ranked lists focus on the top-ranked option. To support this, we need data showing the ranked group spent a disproportionate share of reading time on the #1 museum (the British Museum) compared to the others.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Participants who were not provided with a ranking of the museums spent roughly equal amounts of time reading about each museum.': describes the unranked group, not the ranked group — irrelevant to the conclusion. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Participants who were provided with a ranking of the museums spent disproportionately more time reading about the British museum.': the ranked group spent 36% of time on the #1 British Museum vs. 21% on the #2 option — directly shows focus on the top-ranked choice. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Participants who were provided with a ranking of the museums spent the least amount of time reading about the Natural History Museum.': concerns the third-ranked option, not the top-ranked one the conclusion is about. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Participants who were not provided with a ranking of the museums spent the most time reading about the Victoria and Albert Museum.': describes the unranked group, which has no bearing on the ranked-list conclusion. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
